Watch fires burn at the corners, the air is all kennel stink and hay smell, and a wain stands parked mid ward like a sleeping ox. Across open ground the inner keep door waits under its lamp. And along the east wall a barred grating glows faint, with shapes moving behind it. The caravan folk, alive, close enough that you can hear a child coughing. The sound rearranges everything in your chest.

choices.label: Shadow hop the wall line toward the inner door.
Burrow into the hay wain bound for the inner yard.
Take the sentry post over the kennels.
Sound Finchs two short notes and walk across like you belong.
Walk straight across the open ward for the inner door, fast.
Cut through the banked smithy toward the inner door.
Work along to the east grating and the whisper behind it.

nodes.body: You work the wall line shadow to shadow, and at the cell grating a drover's eyes meet yours and go wide as cart wheels. You put a finger to your lips. Hope travels through the iron bars in both directions, silent and enormous. Then come the kennels, and a dozen wet noses swing up together to read the wind, and the wind, unhelpfully, is behind you.

choices.label: Toss the dried eel and jerky along the kennel fence.
Freeze in shadow until the noses settle.
Push past the kennel fence quick and quiet.

nodes.body: You burrow into the hay wain and hold your sneezes while two goblins wheel it grumbling toward the inner yard. Their complaint is the ash robed one's timetable, moonset moonset moonset, like a curse with a calendar. The wain trundles and sways with the party buried in it like winter apples. And through the hay you watch the inner arch swallow the sky overhead.

choices.label: Burrow deep and ride it all the way in.
Slip out in the arch shadow as it passes.
Stay buried and ride the wain wherever it goes.

nodes.body: You take the sentry post stair tread by soft tread, and the one goblin above is yawning at the stars when Sable reaches it. Her work is quick and quiet as folding cloth. An iron cap and a watch horn become trophies, the post becomes yours until the next relief, and the whole ward lies below you, fires and circuits and the long dark line of the wall walk, waiting to be read.

choices.label: Signal all clear down the wall and cross by the stair.
Study the ward from above first.
Sound the captured horn to wave off the far fire.
Ride the well windlass down to the spring gallery.

nodes.body: You put Finch's horn to your lips and sound two short notes, all clear, and the corner fires wave back lazy and unbothered. Then you cross the ward at a porter's trudge, hood down, heart loud enough to wake the kennels on its own. Goblin eyes slide over the company like rain off wax. It is the longest short walk of your life, and the inner door arrives like a held breath released.

choices.label: Make straight for the inner keep door.

nodes.body: The kitchen midden door sits in the wall angle, unbarred, because nobody guards a smell. Some blessed lazy cook has even greased the hinge with pot fat to spare their own ears. The party slips through one at a time into onion dark, boots finding a floor sticky with a century of spills. And a service stair beyond going down into the under keep, cold rising off it like the breath of a well.

choices.label: Climb the service stair to the under keep.

nodes.body: The great stair door stands under its lamp, iron banded and barred from within, but the bar throws its shadow through the gap between door and frame. Sable's knife slides into that gap and walks the bar up out of its brackets a finger width at a time, patient as frost heaving a stone. It comes free without one sound. She eases the door, and the keep's own dark invites you in.

choices.label: Ease the door and slip in.

nodes.body: You cross the inner yard by wain and shadow, letting the unloading noise cover your boots, and gulp a doorway one heartbeat after a torch passes it. Inside, the under keep stair waits, cold and quiet as a held breath, coiling down into the hill. Somewhere below, faint, the child coughs again, and the sound pulls the whole company downward like a hand on a rope.

choices.label: Descend toward the under keep.

nodes.body: You study the ward from above first, and it reads like a page. Fire circuits counted, twelve breaths per round, and the cell grating's guard change marked against the torch by the door. The hay wain's path chalked in memory, arch to arch, and the stair door's bar glinting through its gap, liftable by a patient knife. Knowledge worth the delay, all of it, and the ward none the wiser for being read.

choices.label: Cross by the stair door with the count in your head.
Read the scratches around the inner door first.

nodes.body: One nose wins its argument with the wind, and the kennels go up like a kicked hive. Torches converge from three corners at once, and there is nothing for it but the wall breach, retaken a stride ahead of the pack with the hunt giving tongue behind you. The hills chase you home empty handed, and the last thing you hear from Harrowstone is the dogs, singing their triumph to the moon.

The open ground is going well until a relief guard arrives early to his post, and stops, and looks. The shout and the rush arrive together, and on bare cobbles there is nowhere to be anything but caught. The cells gain four new tenants who came to empty them, and merchant Tallow's face through the grating, watching you marched past, is the worst part of a bad night.

The watch horn sits strange in your grip, and the note comes out long and falling. The hunting call. The whole ward answers it on its feet, fires flaring, kennels boiling, and there is nothing to do but run the wall walk back the way you came with the hunt pouring up the stairs behind you. The breach takes you out a spear length ahead of the consequence, and the hills hunt you home.

Inside Harrowstone at last. The under keep stair coils down into crypt cold, and fresh torch soot streaks the ceiling, proof of traffic below. The child's cough drifts up again, somewhere below and to the east now, a small stubborn sound in all that stone. And under everything, faint as a pulse felt through a wall, comes something else. One low bell note, held and held and held, never quite ending. The seal is already half singing down there, and you start down toward it.

choices.label: Follow the stair down into the under keep.

nodes.body: The stair delivers you to the under keep, and the under keep offers three ways, each breathing different air. West, the passage runs toward the cells, carrying the child's cough and a smell of damp straw. Center, firelight flickers and something enormous snores in what must be the great hall. East drifts incense, and under it a clatter like dice being sorted by the bushel. And beneath all three ways, patient as a sleeping heart, the drums keep their rite time somewhere below your boots.

choices.label: West, toward the cells and the cough.
Center, through the great hall.
East, toward the incense and the boneworks.

nodes.body: The war court spreads below the shuttered gallery, and you press an eye to the gap. Vessk of the Redtooth sits a chair of wagon boards, scarred captains ranged in a half moon before him. And pacing the open floor with the reliquary cradled like an infant walks Malverin the Ash Robed, explaining in a voice like cold water. At moonset the seal sings, the door opens, and the king under the hill rises to serve. Vessk asks the only question with teeth in it. Serve whom. Every drum in the deep answers nothing.

choices.label: Stay and hear the whole plan out.
Mark the guard rotations below and slip out early.
Study Malverin himself, robes, staff, and the sigil he wears.
Ease the shutter wider for a clean look at the whole court.
Angle for a look at the wizards traveling desk.

nodes.body: You stay, and the whole plan unfolds. The reliquary goes to the stone door at moonset. The shaman sings the seal awake, Malverin speaks the waking words from his book, and the barrow king rises bound to the voice that woke it. The Redtooth are to be paid in the dead king's silver, and in the caravan folk. Vessk spits at that last, saying prisoners were never the bargain, and the room goes knife quiet before Malverin smiles it smooth again. You have everything now except time.

choices.label: Slip down toward the crypt stair with the whole shape of it.
Watch Vessks face a moment longer.
Stay for the captains muttered asides too.

nodes.body: You mark the rotations from the shutter gap instead of the words. Torch relays are already flowing down the processional stair by pairs, the war court emptying itself rank by rank toward the deep. Circuits, timings, the gaps between, all of it writes itself into your memory like a stolen map. Whatever happens at the door tonight, you now know exactly how the hill will move around it.

choices.label: Drop to the emptying floor and follow the last relay down.
Drop to the floor before the court fully empties.
Fall in behind the plunder porters crossing the floor.

nodes.body: You study Malverin himself, cold and careful. Ash gray robes far too clean for this hill. A traveling book chained to his belt, and soft shoes that once walked untroubled through a massacre. At his throat, on silver wire, hangs a bone sigil, twin to the shaman's. The man is armored in preparations, layered in them. But his eyes go always back to the reliquary, again and again, like a debtor to a purse he does not own yet.

choices.label: Slip down toward the crypt stair.
Count the wizards pacing like a jailers rounds.

nodes.body: You slip down the service stair as the court breaks up beneath you, captains barking march orders in the yard voice goblins save for underlings. The hill is pouring itself toward the deep like sand through a glass, every passage flowing one direction. The crypt approaches lie ahead of the crowd, but only just, and only if you move now and keep moving.

choices.label: Make for the crypt approaches.

nodes.body: Vessk's face proves worth the wait. Fear sits on it like a badly fitting helm, and the chief keeps glancing at the passage where his own grandmother's wisdom lives, dig not, wake not. Once, strangely, he looks straight at the gallery shutter, as if he felt eyes there and wished them well on their way. You file that look with the other valuables and slip down toward the crypt approaches, spending strength you are starting to miss.

choices.label: Slip down and make for the crypt approaches.

nodes.body: You follow the last relay at lamplighter distance through the emptying halls, close enough to smell torch pitch, far enough to be nothing. The torches shrink to sparks down the processional stair ahead. Then a side arch opens on your right, breathing older air. Offering the quieter crypt approaches to anyone brave enough to leave the light and go down the dark way alone.

choices.label: Take the side arch to the crypt approaches.

nodes.body: One captain below picks your shutter slat to stare at, for no reason but a soldier's luck, and eyes meet through the gap. The moment stretches, snaps. Boots drum up the gallery stair from both ends at once, and the war court acquires an audience it intends to keep. You watch the rest of the planning from much closer, under guard, and the plan works perfectly for everyone who is not you.

You stay for one sentence too many, and the court rises all at once for the march. Every stair and arch fills with ordered ranks in the time it takes to reach the gallery door, and the gallery becomes a trap with an excellent view. The sweep finds you pinned there before the drums even change tempo, and the rite gains four witnesses in chains.

The crypt approaches run under the oldest stone in the hill, and every way down is spoken for. Left, the processional stair rings with marching torches. Right, a spoil chute yawns black and silent. Ahead, the old chapel drop shaft opens above a dry well, rope ready. And past all three, faint and patient and closer than it has ever been, sounds the one low bell note that never ends. The seal is already half singing, and the night is spending fast.

choices.label: Ride the spoil chute down, braking with your heels.
Leap the chute lip and slide it blind.
Climb down the chapel drop shaft by rope.
Shadow the processional stair behind the relays.
Send Sable alone ahead to read the door approaches.

nodes.body: The chute is a child's nightmare of a slide, bone dust and speed and no second thoughts permitted. You learn the heel brake in the first heartbeat and apply it for the rest of the drop. The bottom arrives as a spoil heap in a crypt antechamber, soft as landings go, filthy as they come, and the drums are suddenly very near, felt in the floor as much as heard.

choices.label: Shake off the dust and find the stone door.
Surf the spoil heap the rest of the way down.

nodes.body: The rope runs down the dry well past dressed stone older than the keep above it, courses laid by hands that measured in forgotten spans. Your boots touch a floor of flagstones fitted tight as bookbinding. A passage leads off, breathing cold, straight toward the bell note, and the well's circle of torchlight shrinks above you like a closing eye as you leave it behind.

choices.label: Follow the cold passage to the stone door.
Swing wide on the rope to skip the last courses.

nodes.body: You take the processional stair in the relays' own shadow, close enough to smell the torch pitch, matching their step for sixty pounding heartbeats. At the last landing you peel off into a side vault as the column wheels toward the barrow gallery. Torch glow flooding past the vault mouth like a river past a snag. The stone door lies somewhere beyond that glow, and the singing is louder here.

choices.label: Cut through the side vault to the stone door.
Stay in the columns shadow one landing further.

nodes.body: The chute forks mid slide where no map said fork, and momentum votes before you can. The wrong throat narrows, and narrows, and holds. The hill keeps what it catches, the way it has always kept things, with the drums for company and the bone dust settling slow in the dark around the last of your season.

The column halts mid stair on some captain's whim, and the torches wheel about like startled birds. The relays' shadow shrinks to exactly nothing around four strangers standing on the processional steps with no torches of their own. Taken in ranks, marched in ranks, you end the night in a cell with a view of the rite, which is the cruelest seat in the house.

The west passage runs toward the cells under torch brackets spaced far apart like bad teeth. And the child's cough comes clearer with every step. Then lamplight spills from a guard nook ahead, along with the click of knucklebones and two voices conducting an argument that sounds years old. Between you and the cell block sit two guards, one game, and a stretch of stone with nowhere to hide except the dark between torches.

choices.label: Wait out the game and slip past on the dark side.
Douse the nook lamp with a thrown wet rag.
Walk past purposeful with a stolen spoil barrow.
Rush the nook and put both guards down quiet.
Slip through the laundry vats and the servants way.
Listen at the second door to the strange soft clapping.

nodes.body: You wait out the game and take the dark side of the passage a boot length at a time, curses and clicking bones covering your progress. It is going beautifully. Then one guard rises mid slip, stretching, and shuffles across the passage to fetch small beer from the barrel on the other side, which puts a goblin, a lamp, and your held breath all within one spear length of each other.

choices.label: Melt into the wall niche and let it pass.
Roll under the beer barrel trestle.
Keep slipping past while he draws his beer.

nodes.body: The wet rag arcs true and the lamp dies with a hiss, and the nook erupts into blame, each guard absolutely certain the other elbowed it. The argument acquires structure, then history, then references to previous incidents. You cross the passage in the arguing dark, all four of you. While the case is still being litigated, and the cell block corner receives you like a verdict.

choices.label: Press on to the cell block corner.
Cross upright and quick while they blame each other.

nodes.body: You wheel the stolen spoil barrow past the nook at a laborer's slouch, shoulders dropped, eyes down, the lie walking on wheels. One guard glances up from the game. It sees spoil, and boredom, and nothing else worth interrupting a winning streak for, and it waves you through with a knucklebone still in its fist. The barrow squeaks twice more and delivers you to the cell block corner, heart hammering under the performance.

choices.label: Park the barrow at the cell block corner.
Wheel it straight through to the block without pausing.

nodes.body: You rush the nook, and the crossing takes two heartbeats. Bram's haft and Sable's pommel land together with a sound like a double knock on a door, and both guards fold into the nook like laundry. The knucklebones rattle to a stop on the game board, unfinished. The passage stands quiet, the lamp burns on, and somewhere down the block the child coughs again, nearer now.

choices.label: Bind and gag them and take their keys.
Drag them into the dark cell at the corner first.

nodes.body: The wall niche holds you the way a coffin holds, snug and instructive. The guard trudges past close enough to smell, all wet leather and onion, draws its small beer, and trudges back. The game rejoins itself with a curse and a rattle. You peel out of the niche one muscle at a time and gain the cell block corner on held breath, with the argument resuming comfortably behind you.

choices.label: Study the cell block from the corner.
Whistle the block an all clear as you come.

nodes.body: The trestle shadow is low and rank, and you fold under it as boots pass at eye level in both directions, close enough to count the hobnails. The crawl out the far side takes the skin off both elbows and a year off your patience. But the cell block corner arrives at last, and behind you the game clicks on. Unbothered, arguing about a throw from before you were ever under the furniture.

choices.label: Study the cell block from the corner.

nodes.body: The cell block corner receives you, the passage behind still arguing or still bored, both conditions equally useful. Ahead runs the block itself. Iron doors line both walls, each with its barred grate, and from the second door on the left comes the cough, close now, dry and small and stubborn. Somewhere behind those doors are nine people who wrote letters home.

choices.label: Study the block before you touch a door.

nodes.body: You truss the guards with their own belts and gag them with their own game bag, which Sable insists has a justice to it. From the nook wall comes the prize, a ring of keys heavy as a promise, iron worn bright with use. The cell block corner waits just ahead, and the keys ride your fist like the answer to a question nine people have been asking for two weeks.

choices.label: Study the block, keys ready.

nodes.body: The dark cell at the corner takes the sleeping guards like a cupboard takes brooms. And you leave its door ajar in a friendly fashion, nothing barred, nothing suspicious. The nook behind you now looks merely empty, its lamp burning for nobody, its game interrupted by nothing anyone could name. Housekeeping done, you gain the corner and look down the block at last.

choices.label: Study the block from the corner.

nodes.body: The beer guard turns at exactly the wrong instant, and eyes meet eyes across the lamp spill. There is a moment where you both consider pretending it did not happen. Then the shout goes up, and the whole west wing answers it, and the passage fills from both ends with iron and torchlight. The cells gain four new tenants who came to empty them, filed two doors down from the child they came for.

The barrow wheel finds the one loose flag in the passage, and spoil and bones ring on stone like a dinner gong. The nook guards prove considerably faster than they looked, and the west wing dark is not deep enough to lose them in. Run down within sight of the cell block, you are filed into it instead, and the barrow, righted, carries on without you in the morning.

The cell block stretches away under one grease lamp, nine iron doors in facing rows. A warder sleeps in a chair beneath the key board, one boot up on a stool, snoring in a minor key. The cough comes again from the second door on the left, and through its grate a face appears. Merchant Tallow himself, gray but unbroken, exactly as the vigil described him. His eyes find yours, and his lips shape one word, silent and precise. Moonset.

choices.label: Lift the key board over the sleeping warder.
Work the second doors lock with Sables picks.
Whisper with Tallow through the grate first.
Wait for the warder to roll deeper asleep first.
Send Sable to spring the kennel gate as a diversion.
Lift the block floor grate and go under the cell row.

nodes.body: The key board hangs a hand's width above the warder's hat, and you lift the keys one at a time so that none swing and kiss their neighbors. The chair creaks once, like a question being asked in another room. You wait it out with a key between two fingers and your pulse in your ears. The snore resumes its rhythm, and the ring comes away whole into your palm, heavy as the answer.

choices.label: Open Tallows door first.
Open the far cells first, working toward the cough.
Snatch the whole ring in one quick motion.

nodes.body: Sable's picks talk to the second door's lock in little clicks, a patient conversation with an old and honest mechanism that never learned to expect cleverness. The wards give up their argument one by one. The bolt draws back sweet as a spoon from pudding, and Tallow's hand is already at the gap. Steadying the door against its own eagerness, his eyes fixed on yours through the opening.

choices.label: Open the door and hold the hinge quiet.

nodes.body: Tallow's whisper comes through the grate, dry and precise as his manifests. Nine alive, counting the child. The warder sleeps, but the relief comes when the torch burns down to the ring, so count your minutes. And one thing more, pushed through the iron like a knife handle first. The ash robed one has promised the goblins the whole caravan as payment, the night the door under the hill opens. People as wages, and Tallow lets that sit.

choices.label: Lift the keys and start on the doors.
Ask him what Malverin took to the crypts.
Take Tallows whispered muster of all nine.

nodes.body: The doors open one by one on hinges fed with lamp grease, and the drovers rise stiff and silent, two weeks of straw and patience unfolding into people. A mother lifts the coughing child, and the child's arms go around her neck without waking. Nine faces gather in the block's one lamp, and all of them ask the same question with no sound at all. What now, and it is a very good question.

choices.label: Ready them and plan the out.

nodes.body: You open the far cells first and let the drovers wake their own neighbors, hands over mouths. The practiced hush of people who have rehearsed this in whispers for two weeks. The block fills with standing shadows, stretching, flexing, becoming a crew again. The second door comes last, and the child is lifted still half asleep, the cough smothered gently into a borrowed cloak that swallows her whole.

choices.label: Ready them and plan the out.
Light the block lamps to work faster.

nodes.body: Tallow's answer comes flat with fury. The reliquary of the first seal, abbey freight under his own bond, insured and sealed. An old lock in the shape of a box, he says, and whatever it locks shut is under this hill. He has watched the ash robed one carry it down to the crypts at dusk and back at dawn, and he has heard him read to it at night, soft, coaxing, like a suitor at a window.

choices.label: Lift the keys and open the doors.

nodes.body: The relief guard comes early, the torch not yet burned to the ring, and finds iron doors standing open on an empty block. There is nowhere to put nine people and a cough on ten heartbeats' notice. The alarm bell rings the west wing awake, and by the end of it the block holds more tenants than it started with, and the torch burns down to the ring on schedule, for whatever that is worth.

One key swings into its neighbor with a chime small as a teaspoon on china, and the warder's hand closes on your wrist before his eyes even open. Old reflexes in an old chair. His shout brings the wing down on the block, boots and iron and lamplight, and the last thing the freeing accomplishes is teaching the keep to hang its keys farther from the chairs.

The plan whispers itself together in the empty cell. Nine people cannot sneak past a live ward, but at moonset the keep empties to the barrow for the rite, every spear drawn down to the deep. So they hide until then, armed with the warder's knives, and go out the culvert grate on your signal. Tallow grips your arm hard at the end of it. Find what Malverin fears, he says, and break it. Behind him the drovers are already prying pins in the boneyard drain.

choices.label: Hide them in the dry cistern off the block.
Hide them in the old chapel loft above the boneworks.
Move them now along the west wall to the culvert itself.
Arm everyone and rush the ward gate together, now.
Split the party, half to the gallery, half holding here.
Let Tallow swear the freight oath over the block.

nodes.body: The dry cistern off the block swallows nine people and their breathing one rung at a time. Water jars and the warder's own bread go down after them, and the child falls asleep at last against her mother's shoulder on the way down, mid protest. You ease the lid stone back over the dark with a straw's width of air left at the rim, and the block above stands empty and innocent.

choices.label: Leave them the signal word and go for the court gallery.
Leave Sable to guard them and go on lighter.
Seat the cistern lid full shut to be safe.

nodes.body: The chapel loft opens off the bell rope stair, deep in the dust of a hundred quiet years, and the boneworks clatter below covers nine sets of climbing footsteps completely. The drovers settle among the broken pews like patient ghosts, armed with kitchen knives and freight law, and Tallow posts himself at the rope head where he can watch the stair. The child sleeps. The dust resettles. The loft keeps them.

choices.label: Leave the signal word and make for the court gallery.

nodes.body: You move them now, the whole block in file along the west wall at a crawl, every buckle muffled in rag, every breath budgeted. The culvert grate waits at the wall foot with its pins already loosened, drover work, quick and neat. Nine people fold into the drain mouth dark one at a time, water at their ankles and their hearts in their teeth. And the grate settles back over them like a lid over live coals.

choices.label: Set the signal and climb back for the court gallery.
Push them faster through the drain while the way is clear.

nodes.body: The signal is set and said back twice. One long whistle down the drain at moonset, repeated by a drover to the next hiding place and the next, a thread of breath holding nine lives strung along it. There is nothing more to do for them now except the thing you actually came to do. The court gallery stairs wait ahead, and the drums below have begun, very slowly, to gather pace.

choices.label: Climb to the war court gallery.

nodes.body: Sable stays with the hidden, shrugging like it is nothing, fooling nobody. She lays her three visible knives out in front of her like cutlery at a formal dinner and settles against the cistern lid to wait. The way to the gallery is lighter without her and lonelier by exactly the same amount. And you feel her absence at your right shoulder like a draft through the rest of the wing.

choices.label: Climb to the war court gallery.

nodes.body: The climb back from the culvert runs through the boneyard drain, cold to the knees and dark as the inside of a pocket. Above you the keep has begun to stir, torch relays flowing down toward the crypts in ordered pairs. Moonset gathering the whole hill like a drawn breath. You come up into the under keep with the signal set behind you and the gallery stairs ahead, and no time left to spend twice.

choices.label: Climb to the war court gallery.
Borrow a sconce torch against the drain dark.

nodes.body: The cough comes at the worst turn of the west wall, one small chest heaving in the shadow line while a torch swings around toward the sound. Nine people press flat as paint and it is not enough. The shout goes up, and the whole rescue is marched back to the block at spear point, you among them, and the child cries the entire way, which is somehow the worst part of all of it.

The drovers refuse the wait, and you cannot entirely blame them. Knives up, they make an honest, doomed rush at the ward gate, and the ward answers it with numbers, the way wards always answer. The yard becomes a churn of torches and shouting. The party is swept back out the culvert alone, and the rescue breaks on its own courage, which is the cruelest way anything breaks.

The center passage opens on the great hall of Harrowstone, and the hall takes your breath the wrong way. Roof beams vanish upward into smoke, a bonfire burns in the old king hearth, and goblins sleep in windrows among the benches, dozens of them, snoring in choirs. At the far end a dais rises under a war banner of red teeth, hung above a heap of caravan plunder. Somewhere in all that firelight and sleeping iron runs a path across, for whoever can find it.

choices.label: Cross the hall floor among the sleepers.
Take the smoke loft along the roof beams.
Circle by the armory corridor instead.
Climb to the minstrel boxes along the halls flank.
Check the trophy wall behind the dais stair.

nodes.body: The hall floor is a puzzle of sprawled limbs and dice cups, and bonfire light breathes shadows across it in slow waves. You plan each step two ahead, weight rolling heel to toe, threading gaps in the snoring. Then the puzzle complicates. A champion of some kind, big as Bram and half again, lies rolled against your very path like a log that mutters, and there is no way forward that does not reckon with him.

choices.label: Step the long way around the muttering one.
Step over him between snores.
Nudge him gently out of the path with a boot.
Buy the old hall hound with the last of the jerky.

nodes.body: The smoke loft opens off a wall ladder, and nerve does the rest. The roof beams run wide as plank bridges and slick with a century of soot, the hall crawling below like a dropped anthill made entirely of sleep. Halfway across, voices drift up from the dais, low and urgent, worth stopping for if your knees can spare the stillness. The beam under you neither agrees nor objects.

choices.label: Stop above the dais and listen.
Cross straight over and down the far ladder.
Hurry the crossing at a soot slick trot.

nodes.body: The armory corridor announces itself with goose grease and iron. Racks of spears lean in bad order down both walls, and at the forge glow two smiths argue over a broken chain shirt with the commitment of theologians. Beyond them the armory proper stands open and unattended. The argument shows no sign of resolving this season, but it holds both sets of eyes, which is worth something to the patient.

choices.label: Wait out the argument in the rack shadow.
Slip past while they shout.
Bluff past the smiths as spoil porters.

nodes.body: You take the long way around the muttering champion, threading dice cups and dreaming spears with your heart in your teeth, and leave him to his argument with sleep. It costs sixty extra steps and a year of your life. The dais stair arrives at last in the hearth's blind spot, and you climb out of the sea of snoring with the gratitude of a swimmer finding stairs.

choices.label: Climb to the gallery landing.

nodes.body: You time the step over to his snores, and mid stride the snore stops. One boot hangs in the air above a goblin chest while his eyes move under their lids, hunting something in a dream. Your balance spends itself coin by coin. Then the snore resumes like a tide deciding to come back in, and you finish the step, and the dais stair receives you a lifetime later, legs shaking.

choices.label: Climb to the gallery landing.

nodes.body: The dais voices come plain from above. A scarred captain is telling a shaman that half the band wants to march home, that the diggers hear knocking from the wrong side of the stone door, and that Vessk has promised the ash robed one two more nights and not one bone more. You take that gold, every coin of it, and cross to the far ladder with the words stowed where you keep the survey page.

choices.label: Take that gold and cross to the far ladder.

nodes.body: You cross the beams at a soot black crawl, elbow over elbow, eyes on the wood and never on the drop. The far ladder creaks once under your weight, exactly once, like a roost settling for the night, and no head turns below. The gallery landing receives you above the sleeping hall, and you lie there a moment being quietly proud and entirely filthy.

choices.label: Take the gallery landing.

nodes.body: The argument runs long enough to grow old in, wandering from the chain shirt to past grievances to somebody's mother. Then both smiths stamp off to supper at once, leaving the shirt unresolved on the anvil. And the armory stands open and unwatched with the forge banked low behind them. You count to twenty and move, and the racks receive you like an aisle in a market with no stallkeeper.

choices.label: Go through the armory.
Help yourself to the forge tongs on the way.

nodes.body: You slip past while they shout, crouched under the forge glow, and the near smith turns to spit at the quench trough exactly as you pass behind his heels. Hammer smoke, held heart, one long stride. The armory door takes you in while the argument peaks behind you over whose fault the rivets are, and you breathe again among the racked spears of your enemies.

choices.label: Go through the armory.

nodes.body: The muttering champion mutters awake. One yellow eye opens, finds your boot in the firelight, and considers it with the unhurried interest of something that has never once needed to hurry. Then the hall rises around you in a wave of iron, windrow after windrow. And the wave carries four strangers to the cells like driftwood, past the plunder, past the banner, past every plan you had.

Soot wins its argument with boot leather on the third beam. The wood goes from underfoot, the hall floor comes up through firelight, and the Redtooth learn all at once that they have visitors. Briefly. The fall is the last page of your season, and the hall's snoring resumes before the smoke settles, which is its own kind of epitaph.

The gallery landing hangs above the dais, and the plunder heaps below like a dragon's nursery, wool and tin and silver plate. Among it stand boxes branded with the abbey freight mark, split open, and empty. No reliquary. Only its packing straw, strewn like the aftermath of a birthday. Behind the dais a passage runs away lit fresh, torches still tall, the way Malverin went, and not long ago either.

choices.label: Follow the fresh lit passage toward the war court.
Search the plunder heap for anything that serves.
Pick the newer lock on the under dais strongroom.

nodes.body: The armory of Harrowstone under goblin management is a scandal and a gift. Racks stand askew, and treasure lies for the taking. Oil flasks, a coil of good climbing rope, and drover knives by the sheaf, confiscated from the caravan and dumped like kindling. Everything a rescue wants is in this room except time, and time is the one shelf the goblins keep stocked against you.

choices.label: Take knives and oil and go for the war court.
Sabotage the racks, bowstrings, and spear hafts first.
Strip the armory of everything four backs can carry.
Tip the quench trough and walk out in the steam.

nodes.body: The fresh lit passage smells of tallow and old stone, and voices grow ahead in council rhythm, question and answer, question and answer. Under them runs a colder voice that needs no raising to be obeyed. And the skin on your arms answers it before your ears finish. The war court is in session somewhere ahead, and the passage is carrying you to it like a delivered letter.

choices.label: Close on the voices to the gallery.

nodes.body: The plunder yields a drover's horn on a strap and a tin of lamp oil, both worth their weight. Then, under the packing straw, a wax tablet in a fine hand. A delivery note. The vessel travels at moonset to the door, payment on opening, signed with a single ash gray M. You read it twice. Somebody is expecting to be paid tonight, and the wages are waiting on a door opening that must not open.

choices.label: Pocket the note and follow the lit passage.
Take the whole tablet stack for Hedda.
Set Elowen on the tablets ciphered margins.

nodes.body: You bundle knives and sling oil, and leave the racks standing but lighter, the difference spread across four packs. The armory door eases shut on the forge glow behind you. The corridor beyond runs toward the council voices, carrying its own cold draft, and every stride away from the arsenal feels like walking off with the keep's teeth in your pockets, because you are.

choices.label: Follow the corridor to the war court gallery.
Carry the oil uncorked for speed.

nodes.body: You work quiet sabotage in the rack shadows, patient as moths. Bowstrings nicked to break at the pull. Spear lashings loosened a half turn each, enough to matter and not enough to see. The goblin arsenal turns half against itself under your hands while the forge hisses counterpoint, and when the Redtooth reach for iron tonight, a fair portion of it will reach back.

choices.label: Finish and follow the corridor toward the voices.
Fire the oil rack and be done properly.

nodes.body: The oil rack catches with a whump that lights the corridor both directions, and the alarms begin beating like a second heart. The keep pours toward the smoke from every wing at once. You are swept out a drain mouth by heat and numbers together, coughing, singed. And the keep stands roused behind you with the captives beyond reach for tonight, and perhaps for longer than tonight.

The passage ends at a curtained arch with torch glow burning through the weave, and the council voices come close enough now to count and tell apart. One cold, one graveled, several afraid. The war court sits just beyond the cloth, discussing the night you came to ruin, and the curtain edge lifts at your touch like it has been waiting.

choices.label: Slip through to the court gallery.

nodes.body: The corridor rises by a service stair to a shuttered balcony, and the war court sound swells underneath it, voices and shifting iron and the scrape of a chair of wagon boards. Deeper down, the drums keep their slow rite time, patient as dripping water. You ease the shutter a finger's width, and the whole court lays itself out below you like a map that argues.

choices.label: Ease the shutter and take the gallery.

nodes.body: The second smith turns back for his dropped tongs at exactly the wrong breath, and iron eyes find soft shadow with nothing between them. The forge bell goes flat and fast under his hammer. And the armory corridor fills from both ends with everything it was built to hold. Caught among the enemy's own weapons, you surrender before the arithmetic finishes, which is the only wisdom left in the room.

One more armful proves one too many. A rack tips its neighbor, the neighbor votes with the rest, and the cascade rings through the corridor bright as a festival of dropped cutlery. The hall next door rises to the sound of its own stolen iron. You buy the way out with everything dropped, every knife and flask abandoned, and the night closes owing you nothing.

The east passage carries you toward a clatter like dice being sorted by the bushel, and delivers you to the boneworks. The old chapel has been stripped to a workshop, and goblins sort crypt spoil into baskets under swinging lamps, bone by bone, tally by tally. Beyond the sorting floor hangs a hide curtain painted with teeth, and behind it, if Finch's map tells true, lies the den of the Redtooth shaman. Keeper of wards, and keeper of keys.

choices.label: Work through the boneworks as spoil sorters.
Take the chapel loft and cross above the sorting floor.
Follow the incense smell around by the shrine niches.
Light a heavier pot and move inside the smoke drift.
Slip into the saints crypt behind the boneworks first.

nodes.body: You shoulder baskets and join the sorters, heads down in the lamplight, bones passing hand to hand while the tally chalk squeaks. It is honest work, in its horrible way, and easy to hide inside. But across the floor the foreman goblin has begun counting workers, and then counting them again. And its frown at the arithmetic is the frown of a creature that trusts its numbers more than its eyes.

choices.label: Keep sorting till his count loses interest.
Send Sable to knock a shelf over across the floor.
Drift for the curtain while he counts the far rank.

nodes.body: The chapel loft looks down on the sorting floor through a fog of dust and dove bones. Below, the painted curtain hangs in plain view, the shaman's smoke seeping through it in lazy question marks. A bell rope hole opens in the loft floor, offering a way down behind the shrine niches for anyone with rope and patience, or without patience, which is generally where the trouble starts.

choices.label: Rope down behind the shrine niches.
Watch the curtain first and learn the dens traffic.
Swing down the bell rope hole directly.

nodes.body: The shrine niches run in single file along the wall, old saints defaced with red teeth, incense thick enough to chew. A novice goblin tends the smoke pots with its back turned, humming to itself, ears stuffed with its own music. It is young, for a goblin, and thin, and its hands shake slightly on the pots, which is worth noticing on the way past.

choices.label: Slip past the novice niche to niche.
Wait for it to carry smoke through the curtain, then follow.
Lift the novices bone flute and play the summons.
Corner the shaking novice softly in the niche shadow.

nodes.body: The count loses to boredom, as counts do, and the foreman wanders off to bully a different rank. Your baskets drift the crew toward the curtain end of the floor, casual as furniture being rearranged. By the last pass the hide curtain hangs a spear length away, breathing smoke at its edges. And nobody on the sorting floor has looked at you twice since the chalk squeaked last.

choices.label: Set the baskets down by the curtain.

nodes.body: The shelf goes over in a bright clatter of femurs, Sable's work. And the whole floor converges on the mess and the foreman's language, which improves nobody. The curtain end of the boneworks empties as thoroughly as a yawn. You cross it unwatched while the argument about whose stacking failed enters its second act behind you, and the hide curtain receives you into its smoke.

choices.label: Cross to the curtain in the noise.
Pocket a tally chalk as you cross.

nodes.body: The rope runs smooth from the bell hole to the niche shadow, and your boots find old altar stone without a sound. From here the curtain edge hangs close enough to read the newest tooth painted over a saint's patient face, paint still glossy, brush strokes hurried. The saints watch you pass with their defaced eyes, and whatever they think of it, they have seen worse tenants.

choices.label: Take the curtain edge.

nodes.body: You watch the curtain first and learn the den's traffic like a ferry schedule. A novice in with fresh smoke at intervals. A digger out with a basket, once. Between visits, a long solitary droning from within, rising and falling, the shaman working alone at something that answers him in creaks. You rope down in the longest gap and take the curtain with the timetable still warm in your head.

choices.label: Rope down and take the curtain between visits.

nodes.body: You go niche to niche behind the novice's humming, saints for cover and smoke for a cloak, matching your movement to its swaying pots. The humming never misses a note. By the last niche your lungs burn from breathing shallow, and the curtain edge arrives like a finish line. And the novice tends its pots on, blessed and oblivious, guardian of nothing it will ever know about.

choices.label: Take the curtain edge.

nodes.body: The novice shoulders through the curtain with its pots, and the hide swings wide for one long breath. Firelight and bone racks print themselves on your eyes, an entire den photographed in a blink. Then you are moving, through on its heels, inside the swing of the curtain, and the way in walks open behind a goblin's turned back exactly as Sable always says it will.

choices.label: Follow through on its heels.

nodes.body: The foreman's arithmetic comes out four heavy, and it trusts its numbers. The chalk snaps. The shout goes up, and the sorters swarm like the floor itself stood up, baskets dropping, bones rolling everywhere. The boneworks sorts four new items into the cells before the lamps stop swinging, and somewhere in the count, you are certain, the foreman is smug about it.

The bell rope hole proves wider than the rope is long. The drop finishes on a shrine shelf in a peal of brass smoke pots, a sound like a temple falling downstairs, and the east wing arrives before the echoes do. The way back up is a receding dream by then, and the cells receive you still ringing faintly, like a struck bell yourself.

The den opens through the curtain gap, firelight and bone racks and wardings knotted from sinew and teeth, swinging slow in the smoke. The shaman itself hunches over a scrying bowl, droning at the water in a voice like a wasp in a jar. And there, on a thong at its belt, hangs a sigil of yellowed bone, carved with the same crown the old silver wears. The twin of the seal on the crypt door, if Tam heard true, and one thong's width from being yours.

choices.label: Lift the sigil from the belt while it scries.
Free the caged crow spirit to buy a bargain.
Put the shaman down fast before it can cry out.
Back away, the wards are not worth it.
Push straight through the curtain at a walk.

nodes.body: The theft is a lesson from Sable performed with your own hands. The thong parts on the drone's downbeat, and the sigil rides your palm, still warm from the fire. Then the scrying bowl catches your eye and holds it. In the water, upside down and rippled, four small figures cross a moor at dusk, and yesterday, watching you back. The shaman has been reading your approach for days, and you are standing inside its book.

choices.label: Slip out with the sigil before the water clears.
Linger to read the bowl over its shoulder.

nodes.body: You open the wicker cage instead, and the crow spirit unfolds like smoke deciding to be wings. The shaman spins from the bowl with a hiss, too late by a feather. The spirit speaks in dropped feathers and dust, a voice like dry leaves crossing stone. One debt owed, and name it. And the shaman freezes where it stands, watching its own leverage stretch free in the smoke, its drone dead in its throat.

choices.label: Name the sigil as the debt.
Name safe passage as the debt.
Name the shamans true name as the debt.
Let the nosy spirit gossip past its debt.

nodes.body: You rush the den in a single breath, and Bram's palm ends the drone mid note, gentle as such things go. The shaman folds like a dropped robe. The wardings swing their teeth in the disturbed smoke, chattering softly, deciding, and settle. The sigil hangs there for the cutting, and the scrying bowl ripples on with nobody watching it, showing the moor, the dusk, the four small figures.

choices.label: Cut the sigil free and bind the shaman.
Grab for the sigil first and the shaman second.

nodes.body: You back away, measured and quiet, and the wards go unrisked. The den keeps its droning, the sigil keeps its belt, and the court gallery still waits to be reached with what you know and what you lack weighing about the same in your pack. Not every door needs opening, you tell yourself, most of the way to believing it.

choices.label: Make for the war court gallery.

nodes.body: You slip out through the curtain on the downbeat, and the drone runs on unbroken behind you, singing to a belt one sigil lighter. The bone warm weight rides your shirt like a stolen egg. The boneworks recrosses itself in the sorters' shift change, one crew shuffling out as another shuffles in. And you go with the current, one more basket carrier in the lamplight with the key to everything under your arm.

choices.label: Climb toward the war court gallery.

nodes.body: The crow spirit dips once, like a bow, and crosses the den. The sigil thong parts at a feather touch, and the bone slides across the floor to your boot, polite as a served dish. The shaman watches its own luck leave with the smoke, drone dead, hands loose, and does nothing at all, because there is nothing to be done. The debt is paid in full, and everyone in the room knows the arithmetic.

choices.label: Take the sigil and go for the gallery.

nodes.body: You name safe passage, and the spirit pays it in a wing beat. The den and the boneworks pass you like a held breath, unclutched, unseen, goblin eyes sliding past the company as if you were furniture being delivered. The east wing is behind you before the smoke settles. The sigil stays droning at the shaman's belt, and you carry the lack of it like a stone in your boot all the way to the stairs.

choices.label: Take the gallery stairs.

nodes.body: The sigil cuts free, and the shaman goes into its own wardings. Bound and gagged and tucked behind the bone racks like a rolled carpet. The den drones on to nobody, the bowl ripples for an empty stool, and the gallery stairs wait ahead. Elowen looks back once at the racked bones and whispers an apology to the room in general, for the mess, which is very Elowen.

choices.label: Climb toward the war court gallery.
Question the bound shaman before you go.

nodes.body: The way to the gallery runs back through the shrine niches, incense trading places with torch smoke a breath at a time. The drums from below have found your pulse now and keep their slow rite time inside it, whether you want them there or not. The defaced saints watch you go, patient as ever, and the gallery stairs take you up toward the voices that matter.

choices.label: Climb to the war court gallery.

nodes.body: The scrying water clears at the worst possible moment, and shows the shaman exactly what stands behind it. The drone becomes a shriek that carries through stone like a nail through wax, and the wards snap their teeth in the smoke all around you. The east wing folds in on the den with you inside it, and the bowl watches the whole capture from its stand, rippling gently.

The shaman is faster than a hunched back promises. A bone dart flicks from the smoke, no louder than a snapped twig, and a numbness spreads from your neck like spilled water finding its level. The den tilts sideways with terrible slowness. The drone is the last sound the dark keeps for you, patient and low, singing something old to the bottom of the scrying bowl.

You push through the curtain at a walk, and one warding brushes your shoulder at the edge. Its teeth chatter like a struck cup, and every knotted charm in the den answers in chorus, sinew and bone applauding the intrusion. The retreat through the boneworks is run at a rout with the alarm chewing your heels. And the east wing stays roused behind you for the rest of the night.

The crypt antechamber ends at the edge of torchlight, and beyond it opens the barrow gallery, a hall older than everything above it. Pillars stand ranked like a stone forest, and at the heart of them waits the great sealed door. The reliquary already sits on a trestle before it. Drummers line a side gallery, the Redtooth pour in from the processional stair, and Malverin unchains his book with the care of a man opening a wound. Under it all the one low bell note swells like water behind a dam. Moonset is close enough to taste.

choices.label: Open the wardens postern with the bone sigil.
Go down the long stair through the pillar shadows.
Sound Finchs calls and turn the Redtooth against the wizard.
Climb through the drummers gallery above the floor.

nodes.body: The bone sigil warms in your fist beside a blank wall panel, and finds its slot the way a key finds a lock in the dark. Stone swallows bone with a sound like a sigh. A warden's passage opens before you, narrow and black and unbreathed for centuries, running behind the gallery wall toward the heart of the barrow. The air tastes of dust and old duty, and the drums come through the stone like a heartbeat that is not yours.

choices.label: Follow the passage at a hunters trot.
Read the warden carvings as you go.
Widen a spy slot for a proper look first.
Search the wardens armory niche as you pass.
Study the choir murals where the spy slots pause.

nodes.body: The passage runs striped with spy slots, and the rite assembles itself in flashes as you pass. Drummers, ranks, the trestle, Malverin's open book, each slice of torchlight a page of the same bad story. Then the passage ends at a postern set behind the sealed door itself, close enough to the trestle's shadow to touch it. The wardens built themselves a back way to the one door that matters.

choices.label: Take the postern to the doors blind side.
Stop at the hinge slot and learn the singers breath.

nodes.body: The carvings run the passage wall like a warden's catechism, cut in the old strokes. The king below did not die, he was stopped, and the seal is not a lock, it is a lullaby. And the last stroke, cut deepest of all, reads like a hand on your shoulder, and wake him for nothing, it says, for no crown is worth the winter he brings. You stand in the dark with the truth of the hill, and the drums keep time beyond the wall.

choices.label: Carry that truth to the doors blind side.
Hunt the carvings for the seals own weakness first.
Pry at the deepest carving for the crown behind it.
Study the last mural panel a breath longer.

nodes.body: The door's blind side is stone cold as a dead hearth, and the postern crack gives you the trestle and the reliquary a spear length away. On the far side of everything you came to stop, Malverin's voice begins the waking words, patient and dry, like frost explaining itself to a window. The seal note climbs a step. Whatever you are going to do, the night wants it done now.

choices.label: Move on the rite.
Widen the postern crack for a better view first.

nodes.body: The weakness is written plain, in a warden's honest hand. The seal sings itself, it needs no wizard. But a wrong note breaks the verse. Strike the reliquary while it sings, and the song starts over from silence, every verse of it, from the very first breath. You carry the knowledge to the door's blind side like a knife carried point down, careful and ready, while the waking words begin beyond the stone.

choices.label: Move on the rite with the knife of it.

nodes.body: You widen one spy slot with impatient fingers, and mortar dust sifts out into torchlight on the gallery side. A goblin face turns up at exactly the wrong moment. The warden's passage becomes a stone sleeve with both ends held, and you are taken out of it like a ferret from a burrow, blinking, furious, and caught.

The long stair hugs the gallery wall, and pillar shadows stripe every landing like the bars of a patient cage. Below, the Redtooth ranks face the door with their backs to you, rank on rank of spears. And far down at the bottom the trestle glows in its own lamp ring, the only lit stage in a dark theater, with the reliquary as the single prop. Every way down is a wager, and the drums are counting the stakes.

choices.label: Descend shadow to shadow between drumbeats.
Take the maintenance ledge behind the pillar tops.
Join the last rank of the procession itself.
Take the pillar forest floor on your belly.

nodes.body: The drumbeats make a ladder for footfalls, and down and hold, and down and hold. You descend inside the rhythm, close enough to the ranks to count scars on the nearest necks. Then a captain turns to scan the stair, one heartbeat after your shadow folds into a pillar's. His eyes walk the steps you just left, slow as a paid inspector, while the drums hold the whole night steady.

choices.label: Hold frozen through his sweep.
Flow on during the next beat anyway.
Glide down two flights in one beat to be past him.
Count the lamp ring before the last flight.

nodes.body: The ledge behind the pillar tops is a mason's walk, never meant for boots at speed, a hand's width of dressed stone above the whole assembly. The rite lies below like a map of itself, ranks and trestle and door. The ledge ends at a carved head taller than a house, whose ear is a step and whose shoulder is a drop, straight down into the door's blind shadow.

choices.label: Climb down the carved head.
Run the ledge upright to save time.

nodes.body: The sweep passes over your stillness like light over stone, finding nothing because you have become nothing. The captain turns back to the door, satisfied, bored. You take the last flight inside the next three drumbeats and reach the barrow floor where the trestle's lamp ring begins, close enough now to hear the reliquary's low note under the drums.

choices.label: Move on the rite.

nodes.body: You flow on during the next beat anyway, and it costs a scrape of boot on grit. One ear below twitches. You shove the breath back down your own throat and hold, one shadow among shadows, until the ear forgets. The barrow floor arrives at last with your heart trying to leave through your teeth, and the lamp ring glowing a spear cast ahead.

choices.label: Move on the rite.

nodes.body: The carved head bears you down feature by feature. Ear, jaw, shoulder, each hold older than any kingdom you can name. The drop lands soft on old dust in the door's blind shadow, and the waking words begin somewhere above your head, dry and patient, spoken to stone that has waited long enough to be patient too.

choices.label: Move on the rite.
Take the carved faces front in the open instead.

nodes.body: One rank wheels on some order you never heard, and torchlight finds you mid stair with nowhere left to fold. The long stair becomes a short parade. You are walked down every remaining step in a cage of spears and presented at the trestle like a gift, and Malverin looks up from his book with the mild interest of a man handed extra ink.

The mason's walk sheds its edge under one boot, and the pillar top tilts away into torch glow. The barrow floor rises through drum time, beat and beat and beat. The rite pauses one beat for the sound, no more, and goes on without you, and the drums close over the place where you fell.

The horn sits cold at your lips in a side vault off the gallery. Finch's calls, learned beside a wrecked wain a lifetime ago, now aimed like a weapon. Below, Vessk stands apart from the wizard with his honor guard around him, a chief watching his own band being spent on another man's war, and not enjoying the arithmetic. The Redtooth obey their horns the way rivers obey their banks. One right note could move an army. One wrong one could end you.

choices.label: Sound the hunt loosed call, pointed at the wizard.
Sound the all clear, then walk to Vessk openly.
Sound the calls at half breath to stay quiet about it.
Approach the honor guard captain under a plain horn.

nodes.body: The long falling note rolls through the gallery like cold water, and every goblin head lifts at once. Hunt loosed. Hunted where. Ranks ripple, spears swing to face four directions, and eyes find Malverin at his trestle one suspicious heartbeat before the captains start shouting orders that contradict each other. The rite's clean machine grinds on sand you poured into it, and the night belongs, briefly, to whoever keeps their head.

choices.label: Slip down through the confusion toward the trestle.
Sound it again from a new vault to feed the panic.
Sound the hunt call a third time for good measure.
Harvest the confusion for gear and watchwords.

nodes.body: The all clear buys you a corridor of doubt, and you walk it openly, palms shown. Vessk's honor guard parts on his flat gesture. The chief looks you over like a wagon he might buy, unhurried, missing nothing. Then he speaks in trade tongue, low and even, and his grandmother forbade this hill to her whole line. So what, he asks, do a dead king's wardens want with a living chief, and the question is a door standing open.

choices.label: Tell him the truth, the wizard will feed the Redtooth to the barrow.
Offer him the caravan plunder to march home now.
Demand he stand his band down in the marshals name.
Press Vessk for hostages and weapons both.

nodes.body: The confusion becomes a moving forest of spears, every point looking outward for a hunt that does not exist. You move through it like weather, unhurried, unremarkable, one more shape in a hall full of shapes. The trestle's lamp ring glows a dozen strides ahead. And the waking words thread on through the noise, patient as dripping water.

choices.label: Move on the rite.

nodes.body: The second call folds the gallery into honest panic. Ranks collide, drummers lose the rite time, and Malverin's voice rises sharp for order in a language goblins do not love. For the space of ten heartbeats the trestle stands unregarded, an island in a churning sea. You spend those heartbeats moving, and the effort costs you strength you no longer have to spare.

choices.label: Move on the rite through the churn.

nodes.body: The truth lands where Vessk's fear already lived. He nods, slow, like a man agreeing with his own grandmother. A murmured order moves the captains like gears, and half the Redtooth simply turn and march for the stair while the wizard's voice climbs behind them, thin with disbelief. Vessk's parting word comes over his shoulder in trade tongue. The door is yours, warden, and we were never here.

choices.label: Move on the rite as the ranks thin.

nodes.body: Plunder weighs against fear in a chief's ledger, and the offer is taken with a spit and a handshake, both binding. Captains peel ranks away by the dozen, laden and cheerful, and the gallery empties around a wizard too deep in his book to count his own army yet. You leave Vessk to his arithmetic and move on the trestle through the thinning ranks.

choices.label: Move on the rite as the ranks thin.

nodes.body: The call cracks on a cold lip, half hunt, half nothing, and every ear in the gallery knows the difference. The side vaults are swept by ranks with torches, methodical as housekeeping, until the horn is found still in your hand. The Redtooth do not love horn thieves, and the rite gains its audience after all.

An honor guard axe turns you back at the last stride, flat of the blade, almost courteous. Vessk shakes his head, slow, final, and some bargains stink worse than barrows, he says, and turns away. And the wizard looks up from his book at the disturbance with eyes like counting weights, and finds the sum of you standing there in the torchlight.

The drummers' gallery is a balcony of skin drums and sweating backs, hammering the rite time out under a ceiling of torch smoke. Scaffold poles and old bell hooks hang overhead like a forest canopy of iron. A drum captain paces the line with a knotted cord, keeping time and discipline both, and neither kindly. The balcony runs the whole gallery's length, and at its far end a stair goes down to the floor and the rite.

choices.label: Cross behind the drummers under the scaffold.
Climb the bell hooks above the smoke.
Shoulder a skin drum and join the relief line.

nodes.body: You pace the crossing to the pounding, each stride swallowed whole by a beat. The drum captain's cord snaps inches from your cheek as he disciplines a lagging drummer, and the drummer takes it without missing a stroke. You are a shadow between thunder and thunder, and the gallery's far stair comes in sight through the smoke, one drum line away.

choices.label: Take the far stair down to the floor.
Cut a scaffold lashing as you pass, for later.
Cross inside the drum line where the smoke is thickest.
Count the rite time from inside the drum line.

nodes.body: The bell hooks make a ladder for hands above the smoke, old iron sound as the day it was set. From up here the whole rite lies mapped below, ranks and trestle, and the door's seal beginning to glow along its carvings like a struck harp string. A hook line runs away and down from your perch, sagging gently, all the way to the pillar tops.

choices.label: Slide the hook line to the pillar shadow.
Climb higher into the smoke for a hook line nearer the door.

nodes.body: You take the far stair in drum time, down into floor dust and spear butts, a forest of legs all facing the wrong way. The lamp ring of the trestle glows ahead through the ranks, and nobody looks behind them, because the door is in front and the door is everything tonight. You thread the forest toward the light.

choices.label: Move on the rite.

nodes.body: One lashing parts with a knife whisper, and the scaffold holds its breath on a single knot now, a lever against the rite for whoever needs one later. You leave it hanging its quiet threat and take the far stair down to the floor. Where the drums walk your heartbeat down step by step ahead of your boots.

choices.label: Move on the rite with the scaffold in your pocket.
Cut a second lashing to be sure of the scaffold.

nodes.body: The hook line hums under your grip, and the slide is fast and blind through smoke that tastes of tallow and old iron. Your boots catch pillar stone in the deep shadow, jarring every joint you own. Somewhere ahead the waking words start, quiet as frost forming, and you spend the last of your wind just holding still to listen.

choices.label: Move on the rite.

nodes.body: The drum captain turns at nothing, at instinct, and the knotted cord points straight at your crossing like a compass finding north. The drums collapse into alarm beat. The balcony is a box with one door, and the door fills with goblins, and the rite below never even pauses while the box is emptied of you.

The smoke runs thicker than any map of hooks. One handhold turns out to be only shadow, and the long reach into it ends the climb. The drummers never miss a beat below as you are carried out of the dark you fell from, and the smoke closes over the hooks as if no one had ever been foolish enough to trust them.

From the door's blind shadow the rite is close enough to touch. Malverin reads the waking words in a voice like frost spreading. The shaman sings to the reliquary, and the seal carvings answer, glowing along the great door line by line. Stone grinds as the door stirs in its frame, an inch of dark opening on older dark, and the bell note swells toward moonset like a tide finding its mark. The whole night narrows to one breath, and in that breath you choose what breaks first.

choices.label: Snatch the reliquary off the trestle mid verse.
Set Elowen and her chalk against the shamans song.
Send Bram to overturn the trestle lamp ring, darkness.
Speak the wizards name from the dark, just his name.
Wait one more verse for a perfect moment.

nodes.body: You snatch the reliquary off the trestle mid verse, and the seal answers with a note gone wrong, a scream of stone on stone. The song collapses around it. The box bucks in your hands like a live thing, furious at the interruption, until the recoil tears it loose to skitter away across the flags, singing its broken note into the dark. And every eye in the gallery turns to find what dared to touch it.

choices.label: Dive behind the pillars as the wizard turns.
Stand and face him over the fallen box.
Chase the skittering box across the open floor.

nodes.body: Elowen's chalk circle flares like a struck match, and her small clear voice threads the shaman's song and snaps it clean in the middle. The drummers lose the time. The seal glow gutters along the door like a candle in a doorway, dimming, failing. And the shaman rounds on the dark with its teeth bared, hunting the counter chant with eyes that have sung to bones for a hundred years and know exactly what a rival sounds like.

choices.label: Rush the trestle while the song is dead.
Leave Elowen to duel the shaman and circle wide alone.
Add your own voice to the counter chant.

nodes.body: Bram hits the trestle like weather. The lamp ring goes over in a sheet of burning oil and sudden darkness, and the reliquary rolls loose across the flags with its low note wobbling. Goblin voices climb over each other in the black, and the waking words stop mid syllable, cut off like a snuffed wick. Somewhere in the roaring dark, the box is rolling, and everyone in the gallery wants it.

choices.label: Hunt the rolling box in the dark by its hum.
Scatter blind into the dark with everyone else.
Kick the burning oil across the processional rugs.

nodes.body: You speak the wizard's name from the dark, just his name, and it lands in the gallery like a dropped blade. Malverin stops mid word. A scholar's vanity turns to find its audience, his eyes hunting the shadows while the seal glow steadies and dims behind him. Then his voice comes, pleasant as a letter opener. Whoever knows him well enough to interrupt, he says, knows enough to be useful, and he offers partnership to the dark at large.

choices.label: Refuse him with silence and move on the trestle.
Step into the light and accept his bargain.

nodes.body: Ash gray light hammers the pillar where you stood a breath ago, and the stone hisses like water on a skillet. The wizard advances on the fallen box with his book held open before him like a shield, reading as he walks. Behind him the gallery folds into rout as the drums die rank by rank. And the night comes apart into running shadows and one patient man in gray.

choices.label: Circle to put the box between you and him.

nodes.body: You face off over the fallen box, his book open, your iron out, and the half woken door grinding its slow inch behind you both. Everything in the gallery that is not already running stops to watch. Malverin studies you the way he studied the reliquary, like a debt he intends to collect. And the seal's broken note hangs in the air between you, waiting to be claimed.

choices.label: Hold his eyes and buy the party position.

nodes.body: You rush the trestle through the dead song, bowl the shaman aside into its own smoke, and knock the reliquary spinning from its cradle to the flags. The box lands singing its wrong note, loud and ruinous. Behind you Malverin's voice goes from frost to fire in the space of one word, and the dark fills with ash gray light looking for something to punish.

choices.label: Turn with the box between you and the wizard.

nodes.body: You hunt the hum through burning oil light and screaming shadow, down on your knuckles among running boots. One hand closes on cold metal at the same instant a soft shoe pins the other end of the box to the flags. You look up the gray robe to the eyes above it, and for a moment the wizard and you are joined by a box that neither of you owns yet. Then you wrench it free, and the effort costs you the last easy strength you had.

choices.label: Wrench it free and give ground.

nodes.body: The perfect moment arrives one verse too late. The seal sings itself open, and the great door swings wide on a cold that puts out torches. Something crowned stands in the dark of it as the waking words end. The hill empties in a rout that carries the party out like flood wrack, alive and unlistened to, and green light burns in Harrowstone all winter, visible from Barrowmere on clear nights, patient as debt.

The ash gray light finds you a half step slow, a hammer of cold that makes no sound at all. The barrow gallery tilts up to meet you, stone and torch glow and dark. The last thing that reaches you is the song starting over somewhere far away, patient, unhurried, as if you had never interrupted anything.

The shaman's answering shriek is older than its wards, and there are teeth in the smoke before you can turn. Sinew charms snap tight around your wrists and your words alike, binding both. The rite resumes behind you as you are dragged to the side gallery, and you watch the rest from your knees, gagged with knotted gut, one witness the song did not need to silence twice.

There are too many hands in your own darkness. Goblin night eyes were always better than yours, and the dark you made belongs to them the moment you make it. The reliquary rolls to a soft shoed stop somewhere out of reach, and the lamp ring is relit around four bound guests of the rite, seated politely where the view is best.

You accept the bargain in front of witnesses who melt away wisely. And Malverin's smile sets like a signature drying. The fine print arrives as the sigil at his throat warms. An apprenticeship of errands and wards and never quite leaving, renewable at his pleasure, which is eternal. The company walks out of Harrowstone without you while the wizard finds you endlessly, endlessly useful.

The whole night turns on this floor. The reliquary lies on the stones between you and the wizard, its song fallen back to silence. The great door stands stuck half a hand open, and something gray gropes patient at the gap, in no hurry at all. Redtooth ranks rout up every stair with their drums dead. Malverin measures you over his open book, unhurried, doing arithmetic of his own. And your body tells you plainly, in the language of burning legs and borrowed breath, exactly how much it has left to spend.

choices.label: Stand, take the box, and end the ash robed ones night.
You are spent, dive for the box and run for your lives.

nodes.body: You make the last stand standing. Bram plants himself a wall on your left, Sable becomes a rumor on your right, and Elowen chalks fast behind you, whispering her letters. The box is snatched to your chest, singing low against your ribs like a cat that does not want to be held. Malverin comes on with ash light gathering in his free hand, slow as weather deciding, and the space between you empties of everything except the next move.

choices.label: Strike the book from his hands.
Give ground and let the groping gap have him.
Rush him bodily with Bram, rope ready.
Race him to the box barehanded, speed against sorcery.

nodes.body: Sable's thrown pommel and your lunge arrive together, and the book goes spinning from his grip, pages fluttering like a shot bird. The gathering light dies unspent in his hand. And the scholar is suddenly only a man in gray, backing away from four angry young faces and one dwarf with an ax, his voice bargaining down through threats to offers to silence. You let him run into the dark and see to your own instead.

choices.label: Let him run into the dark and see to your own.

nodes.body: You give ground a step at a time, drawing him on, until the cold of the gap breathes on your neck and you sidestep like a dancer leaving the floor. The gray hand closes instead on the voice that woke it, gentle as a debt collector. Malverin's last word is the start of a bargain no one stays to hear out. Then the door grinds shut again on its own hunger, and the gallery is very quiet.

choices.label: Stand very still until the stone is quiet.
Crowd him into the gap yourself, hand against hand.

nodes.body: The rush is a drover's answer to sorcery. Bram takes the ash light on his shield boss with a grunt like a struck bell, and then the wizard is borne down under honest weight and drover rope, wrapped to the chin, trussed with his own chained book for a pillow. He curses in three languages and then stops, saving his breath, already planning. You haul him up between you and signal the way out, spending strength you will want later.

choices.label: Haul him up and signal the out.

nodes.body: The scramble is lost by a fingertip. The wizard wins his own weight in vanity back, box and book both, and the ash light herds the party up the dead stair like sheep before a storm. Behind you his laughter closes like a ledger. The hill is lost, the rite only postponed until the next moonset, and the road home is long and full of the arithmetic of failure.

One step too near the patient gap, and the gray grip finds your shoulder instead of his, cold beyond argument, beyond struggle. The gallery goes far away and small, like a lit window seen from a night field. The last thing over the threshold of hearing is the seal starting its lullaby again, and this verse of it is for you.

The dive takes the box on bleeding knuckles, and there is no strength left in you for wizards, only for running. Bram scatters a brazier behind you, Sable calls the turns ahead, and Elowen throws her last chalk to flare blinding white at the wizard's feet. The hill above wakes like a kicked hive, every stair filling with noise. The box hums against your ribs, and your legs ask, reasonably, how far.

choices.label: Run for the culvert line and the drain.
Run for the wardens ways and out the long dark.
Turn at bay in the antechamber arch.
Cut past the half open door itself, the shortest line.
Climb against the rout and take the roofline out.

nodes.body: You run the culvert line on fumes, sending the long whistle ahead down the drain. Iron grates and cold water, hands hauling hands through the dark, nine freed voices counting each other off ahead of you. The ditch takes you all in a tangle under the last of the moon, soaked and shaking and alive, while the keep above roars at its own emptiness like a robbed strongbox.

choices.label: Count heads in the ditch and move.
Stop to wrap the humming box mid run.

nodes.body: The warden's ways swallow the party one by one, and the sigil slot sighs shut on the wizard's curses, cutting them off mid syllable. The stone passages run dark and patient under the hills, older than the Redtooth, older than the keep. Elowen's last candle stub lights spans measured out by dead men. And you walk the long dark out with the box quiet against your back, as if it approves.

choices.label: Walk the long dark out.

nodes.body: The antechamber arch is a fine place for a last stand and a poor one for living. Spears fill it end to end while ash light seals the far arch at your backs. The box is prised from your arms almost gently, a valuable recovered, and the cells close over the company after all, with the rite to be rebuilt by the next moon and a much better guarded trestle.

The shortcut past the half open door is a memory before it finishes. The gray reach is faster than tired legs, and the cold takes the sound out of the world like a hand over a candle. The seal sings you down into the dark it guards, and the song, at least, is gentle.

The way out opens with the box singing low and the wizard finished. Above, the keep is already emptying, Redtooth ranks streaming for the hills with their bargain dead. The cells give up their people into the gray ward, and Tallow counts drovers aloud like a man counting his own heartbeats. The child sleeps at last on Bram's shoulder, small and heavy as a good deed.
